MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number PES-68-184433 Estate of Erwin Hirschfelder.

Appearances: Alfred Dovbish for Suzanne Waterman.

The Court noted that there was no appearance by Mr. Turrini and no one on behalf of the Barbara Hanen Estate. The Court stated that all three Counsel indicated at the last hearing that there was a settlement and that the stipulation never came in. The Court also stated that it needed to adjudicate the accountings, that it would not ask the Trustee to start all over, and that it wants to see Counsel sit down and agree to a number. The matter was continued to March 21, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status of where things stand is to be provided to the Court by March, 16, 2011.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Brown.

MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 184433 The Estate of Erwin M. Hirschfelder.

Appearances: Robert Turrini for Westamerica Bank. Alfred Dovbish for Suzanne Waterman (Primary Beneficiary). Michelle Scannell for Barbara Hannen (Income Beneficiary). Katy Johnson from Westamerica Bank.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Mar-30-2010 Continued To May-18-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number: PES-68-184433 (The Estate of Erwin Hirschfelder).

Appearances: Robert Turrini for WestAmerica Bank (Successor Trustee). Katie Johnson (Trust Officer) from WestAmerica Bank.

Mr. Turrini presented their concerns for the record stating that the veto power isn't being exercised properly. Ms. Johnson stated that some of the their letters get no responses and others get responses which tell them not to act, that they have certain procedures that they need to follow, that the Trust(s) have lost money, that she just took over as Trust Officer a couple of months ago, that there were numerous attempts made by the previous Trust Officer to get Ms. Han, and that Barbara Hanen (Lifetime Beneficiary) doesn't respond to individual securities. The Court stated that it was reluctant to remove the veto power. In addition, the Court stated that if the bank has a plan as to what it would like done that it could submit its proposed action to the Court with notice to Ms. Hanen and her Daughter and then the Court could act. Mr. Turrini requested somewhat of a more affirmative ruling. The Court stated that it would be leaning against that but maybe after some time. The Court continued the petition for instructions regarding veto power to May 18, 2010 at 9:00 am. Counsel is to give thirty days notice.

Judge: Mary Wiss. Court Reporter: Nici Rodich, CSR #11604. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.
